# creditcarddebtbankruptcy.org

> Free guide to filing bankruptcy on credit card debt.

creditcarddebtbankruptcy.org is a free, open-source educational resource about how bankruptcy handles credit card debt. Chapter 7 discharge, Chapter 13 repayment, lawsuits, balance transfers, and rebuilding credit. Part of the Open Bankruptcy Project.

## Pages

- https://creditcarddebtbankruptcy.org/: Can you file bankruptcy on credit card debt? Yes. Credit card debt is unsecured and fully dischargeable in Chapter 7 and partially repaid in Chapter 13.
- https://creditcarddebtbankruptcy.org/chapter-7.html: Chapter 7 and credit card debt. How discharge eliminates credit card balances entirely.
- https://creditcarddebtbankruptcy.org/chapter-13.html: Chapter 13 repayment of credit card debt. How plans handle unsecured credit card balances.
- https://creditcarddebtbankruptcy.org/before-filing.html: What to do before filing bankruptcy on credit card debt. Stop using cards, gather statements, credit counseling.
- https://creditcarddebtbankruptcy.org/credit-after.html: Rebuilding credit after bankruptcy discharge. Secured cards, credit builder loans, timeline to recovery.
- https://creditcarddebtbankruptcy.org/lawsuits.html: When credit card companies sue and how bankruptcy stops lawsuits via the automatic stay.
- https://creditcarddebtbankruptcy.org/balance-transfers.html: Balance transfer traps before bankruptcy. Why transferring balances before filing can create fraud issues.
- https://creditcarddebtbankruptcy.org/secured-vs-unsecured.html: Secured vs unsecured credit card debt in bankruptcy.
- https://creditcarddebtbankruptcy.org/faq.html: Frequently asked questions about credit card debt and bankruptcy.

## Key Facts

- Credit card debt is general unsecured debt, fully dischargeable in Chapter 7
- In Chapter 13, credit card debt is paid through the plan at the same rate as other unsecured claims
- Luxury purchases over $800 within 90 days of filing may be presumed nondischargeable under Section 523(a)(2)(C)
- Cash advances over $1,100 within 70 days of filing may be presumed nondischargeable
- Balance transfers shortly before filing can trigger fraud objections
- The automatic stay immediately stops credit card lawsuits, garnishments, and collection calls
- Most people can rebuild credit to 700+ within 2-3 years after discharge

## Network

Part of the Open Bankruptcy Project:
- 1328f.com -- Discharge eligibility screener
- 1328f.org -- Research platform
- creditcardforgiveness.org -- Credit card forgiveness options
- debtsettlementvsbankruptcy.com -- Settlement vs bankruptcy comparison
- 523a.org -- Nondischargeable debts
- averagecreditcarddebt.org -- Average credit card debt statistics
- meanstest.org -- Chapter 7 means test
- automaticstay.org -- The automatic stay

## Contact

This site is maintained by volunteers. No legal advice is provided.
